transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Good afternoon, First National Bank. My name is Tom. For security, could I take your name and date of birth?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Yes — it's Grace Adeyemi, fifth of May 1990.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you Grace, verified. How can I help you?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: My card has been declined twice this afternoon. I have money in my account — I checked the app — so I don't understand what's happening.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Let me check the card status now. I can see an automatic fraud prevention hold was placed on the card this afternoon following an unusual purchase pattern — two transactions in different cities within a short time window.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Oh — I see. I was in London this morning and then I drove to Birmingham. That must be why.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Exactly right. Can you confirm — were both those transactions yours?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Yes, both mine. The coffee shop in London and the petrol station in Birmingham.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Perfect, I've removed the hold and your card is active right now. I've also added a travel note to your account for the next seven days so this shouldn't happen again.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: That's a relief. Thank you Tom.
